content: Alcohol is a toxin, but at very low doses, it might have indirect health benefits due to hormesis. For example, alcohol shares enzymes with vitamin A metabolism, so less alcohol allows more vitamin A activation. Alcohol causes oxidative stress, downregulates diamine oxidase, and interferes with histamine metabolism. Hormesis suggests small amounts improve defenses against these issues. Observational data shows maximal protective effects of alcohol between 2.5g and 30g per day, about 2.5g per day.
